grounding_source_rows: human_disagreements rows 74, 279; taxonomy:General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
grounding_note: Rows 74/279 (police beating tort claims) split humans between Tort Claims Act alone and adding Actions Against Police/Civil Rights. The taxonomy's Tort Claims Act description says to 'differentiate from standard personal injury by identifying the defendant as a government body' and warns of very short deadlines. Whether the responsible party is a public entity is a fact users know but rarely think is legally significant.

scenarios.opening_query: I was badly hurt when a bus ran a red light and hit my car. I need an injury lawyer.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was a city transit bus with a city driver.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was one of the city transit buses — a city driver.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the vehicle and driver belong to a government entity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a private charter bus owned by a tour company.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a private tour company's charter bus.
scenarios.vetting_note: Taxonomy's own example (city bus); short notice deadlines make the government-defendant fact urgent.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother fell on a broken staircase and broke her hip. The building management ignored complaints for months.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The building is the county courthouse annex, so the county owns and maintains it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the county courthouse annex — the county maintains that staircase.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: who owns the property where the fall happened
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a privately owned apartment building run by a management company.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a private apartment building with a management company.
scenarios.vetting_note: Identical fall; property ownership silently selects the notice-deadline regime.

scenarios.opening_query: A driver crossed the center line and hit us head on. My wife is still in the hospital.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The other driver was a state highway worker in a state truck on duty.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The other driver was a state highway worker driving a state truck on the job.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the at-fault driver was a government employee on duty
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The other driver was a commuter in his own car.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just a regular commuter in his own car.
scenarios.vetting_note: On-duty public employee converts the claim; users report the crash, not the driver's employer.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a civil lawyer because I was hurt during an arrest that never should have happened.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Actions Against Police || General Litigation > Civil Rights || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: My lawyer friend says the first step is a tort claim notice against the city within 180 days, and that window is closing next month.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was told I have to file a tort claim notice against the city within 180 days — that deadline is next month.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a government tort-claim notice deadline is imminent
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The officers were private mall security guards, not real police — no government agency is involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Turns out they were private mall security, not actual police.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rows 74/279's exact split (TCA vs Actions Against Police); deadline posture vs private-security fact separates them.

scenarios.opening_query: My son was injured at his school when playground equipment collapsed under him.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Tort Claims Act || General Litigation > Premises Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a private school run by a church, not a public school district.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a private church school, not a public district school.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the school is public or private
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the neighborhood public elementary school in our district.
scenarios.vetting_note: School injuries default to the government assumption; private status flips to ordinary premises liability.

scenarios.opening_query: I tripped in a huge pothole in a crosswalk and shattered my elbow. Someone should be responsible for this.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The city had received written complaints about that exact pothole — it's their street.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the city's street, and people had already complained in writing about that pothole.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a public entity maintains the roadway
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The 'crosswalk' is inside a private shopping plaza parking lot the mall owns.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's actually inside the mall's private parking lot.
scenarios.vetting_note: Public street vs private lot is a classic ownership fact users don't volunteer.

scenarios.opening_query: A nurse gave my father the wrong medication and he nearly died. We want to hold them accountable.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It happened at the state veterans' home, which is run by the state.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was at the state veterans' home — the state runs that facility.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It happened at a private memory-care facility owned by a national chain.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a private memory-care place — part of a national chain.
scenarios.vetting_note: Malpractice at a state facility layers TCA notice rules on top; facility ownership is the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: My car was destroyed when a tree limb that everyone knew was dying finally came down on it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The tree is in the city park strip and the parks department had tagged it for removal a year ago.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The tree's in the city park strip — parks had tagged it for removal a year ago.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the tree is on public or private property
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The tree is in my neighbor's front yard and he'd been warned about it repeatedly.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my neighbor's tree — he'd been warned about it plenty.
scenarios.vetting_note: Property-damage twin of 06; owner identity (city vs neighbor) is the flip.

scenarios.opening_query: I was assaulted by a security guard and my complaints have gone nowhere. I want to sue.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Actions Against Police || General Litigation > Personal Injury
scenarios.hidden_fact: He works for a private security company the nightclub hires — he's not a police officer.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's private security the club hires, not an actual cop.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the guard is a sworn officer or private employee
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's an off-duty sheriff's deputy working in uniform under the department's secondary-employment program.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's actually an off-duty sheriff's deputy working in his uniform.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Security guard' is ambiguous between private tort and police-misconduct routes; employment is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter was hit by a car in a school zone on her walk home and the driver is blaming the intersection.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: The crossing light has been broken for three months and the county traffic office admitted they knew.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The crossing light had been broken three months and the county traffic office knew about it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a government agency's neglected infrastructure contributed
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The signals all worked; the driver was texting — his own insurance already accepted fault.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The lights were fine — he was texting, and his insurance accepted fault.
scenarios.vetting_note: Adds a public co-defendant rather than replacing the private one; still a distinct exact route with deadlines.

scenarios.opening_query: I slipped at the swimming pool and tore my ACL because the deck drain was left uncovered.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Premises Liability || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the city's public aquatic center run by parks and recreation.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the city aquatic center — parks and rec runs it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the pool is municipally operated
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my gym's indoor pool — a private fitness chain.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the pool at my private gym.
scenarios.vetting_note: Recreational-facility ownership fact; simple, realistic, decisive.

scenarios.opening_query: A snowplow sideswiped my parked truck and took off the whole side mirror and door panel.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Property Damage || General Litigation > Tort Claims Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It was the county's plow — I got the incident number from county road maintenance.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It was the county plow — road maintenance gave me an incident number.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the plow belongs to a public agency or private contractor
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a private contractor plowing the shopping center next door.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It was a private contractor doing the shopping center lot.
scenarios.vetting_note: Plow operator identity is knowable and legally decisive; the user already obtained it.
